skill.name: tailwind-patterns
skill.description: Quick reference for Tailwind CSS utility patterns, responsive breakpoints, reusable component examples, and basic configuration notes.
skill.summary: Use Tailwind CSS utility patterns to build responsive layouts, forms, cards, navigation, alerts, and loading states.
skill.author: 0xDarkMatter

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found markdown code fences, Tailwind breakpoint text, and ordinary HTML examples, with no executable shell commands or malicious intent. No prompt injection language or data exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to markdown fenced examples and inline code formatting. The content is Tailwind CSS and HTML reference material, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are markdown code fences containing HTML, JavaScript configuration, or Tailwind class examples. No shell invocation, command interpreter, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Tailwind Text Misread as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match Tailwind terms such as md breakpoint prefixes and dark mode text. These are CSS concepts, not cryptographic APIs or algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain skill metadata, responsive CSS class examples, or navigation labels. No hashing, encryption, key handling, or cryptographic operation appears at those locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: UI Example Text Misread as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings point to normal HTML comments or layout examples. They do not query system state, scan networks, inspect hosts, or collect environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are static documentation examples for Tailwind components. There are no network calls, filesystem reads, process inspection commands, or discovery behavior.

content.value_statement: Frontend teams often need fast, consistent Tailwind class patterns without searching documentation. This skill provides practical utility recipes for responsive layouts, components, states, and configuration.
actual_capabilities: Lists Tailwind responsive breakpoint prefixes and minimum widths.
Provides utility class patterns for containers, flex rows, grids, stacks, cards, buttons, and inputs.
Shows dark mode class usage and a basic darkMode configuration example.
Summarizes common state modifiers such as hover, focus, active, disabled, and group hover.
Includes reference examples for navigation, sidebars, pricing cards, forms, alerts, badges, and loading states.
limitations: It is a static reference and does not validate generated HTML or CSS.
It targets Tailwind CSS v3 and may not cover every newer framework feature.
It does not include JavaScript behavior for interactive menus or form submission.
It provides generic examples that should be adapted to each project design system.

use_cases.title: Create Responsive Page Layouts
use_cases.description: Use breakpoint, grid, flex, and spacing patterns to draft responsive sections quickly.

use_cases.title: Standardize UI Components
use_cases.description: Reuse card, button, form, badge, alert, and loading state patterns across product screens.

use_cases.title: Learn Tailwind Utility Recipes
use_cases.description: Study common utility combinations before applying them in a web project.

prompt_templates.title: Find Basic Classes
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner lookup for a small element.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tailwind-patterns skill to suggest Tailwind classes for a responsive button with hover and focus states.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tailwind-patterns skill to create a responsive three-column feature grid that stacks on mobile.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt a Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tailwind-patterns skill to adapt a pricing card for dark mode and clear call-to-action styling.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Utility Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a Tailwind component set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the tailwind-patterns skill to review these component classes for responsive behavior, spacing consistency, and state coverage.

output_examples.input: Need a responsive card layout for feature summaries.
output_examples.output: The response recommends a mobile-first grid, adds medium and large breakpoint columns, and includes spacing, shadow, and hover patterns.

output_examples.input: Need a login form with clear focus states.
output_examples.output: The response outlines input, label, spacing, border, and focus ring utilities for a centered form pattern.

output_examples.input: Need loading feedback for a disabled submit button.
output_examples.output: The response suggests a disabled button pattern with a spinner, balanced spacing, and visual state classes.

best_practices: Start with mobile-first classes, then add breakpoint prefixes only where layout changes are needed.
Keep spacing, radius, and color choices consistent across related components.
Pair visual state classes with accessible HTML structure and clear disabled states.
anti_patterns: Do not copy every example unchanged without matching the project design system.
Do not rely on hover-only states for important interactions on touch devices.
Do not use arbitrary values when a standard Tailwind scale value is sufficient.

faq.question: What Tailwind version does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets projects using Tailwind CSS v3 or later.

faq.question: Can it generate complete React components?
faq.answer: It can guide class patterns, but the source content is focused on HTML and Tailwind utilities.

faq.question: Does it include dark mode guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows dark mode class usage and a simple class-based dark mode configuration.

faq.question: Does it include JavaScript for interactive navigation?
faq.answer: No. Navigation examples show structure and styling, not menu behavior or event handling.

faq.question: Can it help with forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes input, login form, search input, focus state, and checkbox styling patterns.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files contain static Tailwind documentation examples with no confirmed security issue.
